name: htmldate

metadata.name: htmldate
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Fast and robust extraction of original and updated publication dates from URLs and web pages.

documentation:
# Htmldate: Find the Publication Date of Web Pages [![Python package](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/htmldate.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/htmldate) [![Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/htmldate.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/htmldate) [![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/htmldate/badge/?version=latest)](https://htmldate.readthedocs.org/en/latest/?badge=latest) [![Code Coverage](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/adbar/htmldate.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/adbar/htmldate) [![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/htmldate?color=informational)](https://pepy.tech/project/htmldate) [![JOSS article reference DOI: 10.21105/joss.02439](https://img.shields.io/badge/JOSS-10.21105%2Fjoss.02439-brightgreen)](https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.02439)

Find **original and updated publication dates** of any web page. **On the command-line or with Python**, all the steps needed from web page download to HTML parsing, scraping, and text analysis are included. The package is used in production on millions of documents and integrated by [multiple libraries](https://github.com/adbar/htmldate/network/dependents). ## In a nutshell

### With Python ``` python >>> from htmldate import find_date >>> find_date('http://blog.python.org/2016/12/python-360-is-now-available.html') '2016-12-23' ``` ### On the command-line ``` bash $ htmldate -u http://blog.python.org/2016/12/python-360-is-now-available.html '2016-12-23' ``` ## Features - Flexible input: URLs, HTML files, or HTML trees can be used as input (including batch processing). - Customizable output: Any date format (defaults to [ISO 8601 YMD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)). - Detection of both original and updated dates. - Multilingual. - Compatible with all recent versions of Python. ### How it works Htmldate operates by sifting through HTML markup and if necessary text elements. It features the following heuristics: 1. **Markup in header**: Common patterns are used to identify relevant elements (e.g. `link` and `meta` elements) including [Open Graph protocol](http://ogp.me/) attributes. 2. **HTML code**: The whole document is searched for structural markers like `abbr` or `time` elements and a series of attributes (e.g. `postmetadata`). 3. **Bare HTML content**: Heuristics are run on text and markup: - In `fast` mode the HTML page is cleaned and precise patterns are targeted. - In `extensive` mode all potential dates are collected and a disambiguation algorithm determines the best one. Finally, the output is validated and converted to the chosen format. ## Performance 1000 web pages containing identifiable dates (as of 2023-11-13 on Python 3.10) | Python Package | Precision | Recall | Accuracy | F-Score | Time | | -------------- | --------- | ------ | -------- | ------- | ---- | | articleDateExtractor 0.20 | 0.803 | 0.734 | 0.622 | 0.767 | 5x | | date_guesser 2.1.4 | 0.781 | 0.600 | 0.514 | 0.679 | 18x | | goose3 3.1.17 | 0.869 | 0.532 | 0.493 | 0.660 | 15x | | htmldate\[all\] 1.6.0 (fast) | **0.883** | 0.924 | 0.823 | 0.903 | **1x** | | htmldate\[all\] 1.6.0 (extensive) | 0.870 | **0.993** | **0.865** | **0.928** | 1.7x | | newspaper3k 0.2.8 | 0.769 | 0.667 | 0.556 | 0.715 | 15x | | news-please 1.5.35 | 0.801 | 0.768 | 0.645 | 0.784 | 34x | For the complete results and explanations see [evaluation page](https://htmldate.readthedocs.io/en/latest/evaluation.html). ## Installation Htmldate is tested on Linux, macOS and Windows systems, it is compatible with Python 3.8 upwards. It can notably be installed with `pip` (`pip3` where applicable) from the PyPI package repository: - `pip install htmldate` - (optionally) `pip install htmldate[speed]` The last version to support Python 3.6 and 3.7 is `htmldate==1.8.1`. ## Documentation For more details on installation, Python & CLI usage, **please refer to the documentation**: [htmldate.readthedocs.io](https://htmldate.readthedocs.io/) ## License This package is distributed under the [Apache 2.0 license](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html). Versions prior to v1.8.0 are under GPLv3+ license. ## Author This project is part of methods to derive information from web documents in order to build [text databases for research](https://www.dwds.de/d/k-web) (chiefly linguistic analysis and natural language processing). Extracting and pre-processing web texts to meet the exacting standards is a significant challenge. It is often not possible to reliably determine the date of publication or modification using either the URL or the server response. For more information: [![JOSS article reference DOI: 10.21105/joss.02439](https://img.shields.io/badge/JOSS-10.21105%2Fjoss.02439-brightgreen)](https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.02439) [![Zenodo archive DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.3459599](https://img.shields.io/badge/DOI-10.5281%2Fzenodo.3459599-blue)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3459599) ``` shell @article{barbaresi-2020-htmldate, title = {{htmldate: A Python package to extract publication dates from web pages}}, author = "Barbaresi, Adrien", journal = "Journal of Open Source Software", volume = 5, number = 51, pages = 2439, url = {https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.02439}, publisher = {The Open Journal}, year = 2020, } ``` - Barbaresi, A. \"[htmldate: A Python package to extract publication dates from web pages](https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.02439)\", Journal of Open Source Software, 5(51), 2439, 2020. DOI: 10.21105/joss.02439 - Barbaresi, A. \"[Generic Web Content Extraction with Open-Source Software](https://hal.archives-ouvertes.fr/hal-02447264/document)\", Proceedings of KONVENS 2019, Kaleidoscope Abstracts, 2019. - Barbaresi, A. \"[Efficient construction of metadata-enhanced web corpora](https://hal.archives-ouvertes.fr/hal-01371704v2/document)\", Proceedings of the [10th Web as Corpus Workshop (WAC-X)](https://www.sigwac.org.uk/wiki/WAC-X), 2016. You can contact me via my [contact page](https://adrien.barbaresi.eu/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/adbar). ## Contributing [Contributions](https://github.com/adbar/htmldate/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) are welcome as well as issues filed on the [dedicated page](https://github.com/adbar/htmldate/issues). Special thanks to the [contributors](https://github.com/adbar/htmldate/graphs/contributors) who have submitted features and bugfixes! ## Acknowledgements Kudos to the following software libraries: - [lxml](http://lxml.de/), [dateparser](https://github.com/scrapinghub/dateparser) - A few patterns are derived from the [python-goose](https://github.com/grangier/python-goose), [metascraper](https://github.com/ianstormtaylor/metascraper), [newspaper](https://github.com/codelucas/newspaper) and [articleDateExtractor](https://github.com/Webhose/article-date-extractor) libraries. This module extends their coverage and robustness significantly.

modules.name: htmldate.validators
documentation: Filters for date parsing and date validators.
pylint:disable-msg=E0611,I1101

functions.name: htmldate.validators.is_valid_date
documentation: Validate a string w.r.t. the chosen outputformat and basic heuristics

functions.name: htmldate.validators.is_valid_format
documentation: Validate the output format in the settings

functions.name: htmldate.validators.plausible_year_filter
documentation: Filter the date patterns to find plausible years only

functions.name: htmldate.validators.compare_values
documentation: Compare the date expression to a reference

functions.name: htmldate.validators.filter_ymd_candidate
documentation: Filter free text candidates in the YMD format

functions.name: htmldate.validators.convert_date
documentation: Parse date and return string in desired format

functions.name: htmldate.validators.check_extracted_reference
documentation: Test if the extracted reference date can be returned

functions.name: htmldate.validators.get_min_date
documentation: Validates the minimum date and/or defaults to earliest plausible date

functions.name: htmldate.validators.get_max_date
documentation: Validates the maximum date and/or defaults to latest plausible date

modules.name: htmldate.utils
documentation: Module bundling functions related to HTML processing.
pylint:disable-msg=E0611,I1101

functions.name: htmldate.utils.isutf8
documentation: Simple heuristic to determine if a bytestring uses standard unicode encoding

functions.name: htmldate.utils.detect_encoding
documentation: Read all input or first chunk and return a list of encodings

functions.name: htmldate.utils.decode_file
documentation:
Guess bytestring encoding and try to decode to Unicode string.
Resort to destructive conversion otherwise.

functions.name: htmldate.utils.decode_response
documentation:
Read the urllib3 object corresponding to the server response, then
try to guess its encoding and decode it to return a unicode string

functions.name: htmldate.utils.fetch_url
documentation:
Fetches page using urllib3 and decodes the response.

Args:
    url: URL of the page to fetch.

Returns:
    HTML code as string, or Urllib3 response object (headers + body), or empty string in case
    the result is invalid, or None if there was a problem with the network.

functions.name: htmldate.utils.load_html
documentation:
Load object given as input and validate its type
(accepted: lxml.html tree, bytestring and string)

methods.name: htmldate.utils.Extractor.__init__
documentation: consider dataclasses for Python 3.7+

modules.name: htmldate
documentation: Htmldate extracts original and updated publication dates from URLs and web pages.

documentation: Meta-functions to be applied module-wide.

functions.name: htmldate.meta.reset_caches
documentation:
Reset all known LRU caches used to speed-up processing.
This may release some memory.

documentation: Implementing a basic command-line interface.

functions.name: htmldate.cli.cli_examine
documentation: Generic safeguards and triggers

functions.name: htmldate.cli.parse_args
documentation: Define parser for command-line arguments

functions.name: htmldate.cli.process_args
documentation: Process the arguments passed on the command-line.

functions.name: htmldate.cli.main
documentation: Run as a command-line utility.

modules.name: htmldate.settings
documentation: Listing a series of settings that are applied module-wide.
pylint:disable-msg=E0611

modules.name: htmldate.extractors
documentation: Custom parsers and XPath expressions for date extraction
pylint:disable-msg=E0611,I1101

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.discard_unwanted
documentation: Delete unwanted sections of an HTML document and return them as a list

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.extract_url_date
documentation: Extract the date out of an URL string complying with the Y-M-D format

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.correct_year
documentation: Adapt year from YY to YYYY format

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.try_swap_values
documentation: Swap day and month values if it seems feasible.

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.regex_parse
documentation:
Try full-text parse for date elements using a series of regular expressions
with particular emphasis on English, French, German and Turkish

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.custom_parse
documentation: Try to bypass the slow dateparser

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.external_date_parser
documentation: Use dateutil parser or dateparser module according to system settings

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.try_date_expr
documentation: Use a series of heuristics and rules to parse a potential date expression

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.img_search
documentation: Skim through image elements

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.json_search
documentation: Look for JSON time patterns in JSON sections of the tree

functions.name: htmldate.extractors.idiosyncrasies_search
documentation: Look for author-written dates throughout the web page

variables.name: htmldate.extractors.REGEX_MONTHS
documentation: todo: check "août"

modules.name: htmldate.core
documentation:
Module bundling all functions needed to determine the date of HTML strings
or LXML trees.
pylint:disable-msg=E0611,I1101

functions.name: htmldate.core.logstring
documentation: Format the element to be logged to a string.

functions.name: htmldate.core.examine_date_elements
documentation: Check HTML elements one by one for date expressions

functions.name: htmldate.core.examine_header
documentation:
Parse header elements to find date cues

```
param tree

LXML parsed tree object

type tree

LXML tree

param options

Options for extraction

type options

Extractor

return

Returns a valid date expression as a string, or None
```

functions.name: htmldate.core.select_candidate
documentation: Select a candidate among the most frequent matches

functions.name: htmldate.core.search_pattern
documentation: Chained candidate filtering and selection

functions.name: htmldate.core.compare_reference
documentation: Compare candidate to current date reference (includes date validation and older/newer test)

functions.name: htmldate.core.examine_abbr_elements
documentation: Scan the page for abbr elements and check if their content contains an eligible date

functions.name: htmldate.core.examine_time_elements
documentation: Scan the page for time elements and check if their content contains an eligible date

functions.name: htmldate.core.normalize_match
documentation:
Normalize string output by adding "0" if necessary,
and optionally expand the year from two to four digits.

functions.name: htmldate.core.search_page
documentation:
Opportunistically search the HTML text for common text patterns

```
param htmlstring

The HTML document in string format, potentially cleaned and stripped to
the core (much faster)

type htmlstring

string

param options

Define extraction options

type options

Extractor

return

Returns a valid date expression as a string, or None
```

functions.name: htmldate.core.find_date
documentation:
Extract dates from HTML documents using markup analysis and text patterns

```
param htmlobject

Two possibilities: 1. HTML document (e.g. body of HTTP request or .html-file) in text string
form or LXML parsed tree or 2. URL string (gets detected automatically)

type htmlobject

string or lxml tree

param extensive_search

Activate pattern-based opportunistic text search

type extensive_search

boolean

param original_date

Look for original date (e.g. publication date) instead of most recent
one (e.g. last modified, updated time)

type original_date

boolean

param outputformat

Provide a valid datetime format for the returned string
(see datetime.strftime())

type outputformat

string

param url

Provide an URL manually for pattern-searching in URL
(in some cases much faster)

type url

string

param verbose

Set verbosity level for debugging

type verbose

boolean

param min_date

Set the earliest acceptable date manually (ISO 8601 YMD format)

type min_date

datetime, string

param max_date

Set the latest acceptable date manually (ISO 8601 YMD format)

type max_date

datetime, string

param deferred_url_extractor

Use url extractor as backup only to prioritize full expressions,
e.g. of the type 

type deferred_url_extractor

boolean

return

Returns a valid date expression as a string, or None
```
